item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article has been replaced with this image and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: Re: Over the Garden Wall
item.content:encoded:
This was posted in February 2012. Over the Garden Wall aired in November 2014. So this very clearly isn't derivative.

The idea of an entity trapped in a flame is a very old one, and for example is a common theme in the lore of jinn in Islamic cultures.

item.title: Over the Garden Wall
item.content:encoded:
This has a striking resemblance to the lantern in over the garden wall…

also:
1-sorry for coldposting
2-more constructive critique coming soon, just wanted to mention this as to make sure we haven't infringed on any idea

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, I kinda wanna make one.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Short, sweet, and gives me the creeps; I like it! Here's a read I put together, I hope I did it justice:

https://youtu.be/VKnD1ncqfLg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to have to disagree. The SCP Foundation doesn't just contain things that people need to worry about. If something is anomalous, even something as simple as this, it will be contained. Protecting human knowledge is just as important as protecting humanity as a whole. But there is more going on in this SCP than in an anomalous item. This tells a story, albeit in very broad strokes, and anomalous items don't do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an anomalous item at best. There's nothing to contain, there's nothing to be worried about, there's more fit for human life in the deli of a Walmart than this article

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hey, no problem. Timer has been removed, as has the crosslink tag. Thanks for the response and letting us know.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I would rather this one be left alone.

Edit: that said, if you want to link to it, that's okay.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Removed. No edits made per Author Request.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This article packs quite a punch considering its brevity. Any cross links that are added, therefore, should not be overly long so as to bog the article down. One simple crosslink would be a proposal for researchers to have someone use SCP-2814's fire manipulation properties on a lit flame within SCP-1045 to check its effects on the shadows. As such, I am recommending the following addition below:

Adding this:

Addendum 2: Proposals to test the effects of SCP-2814's flame manipulation properties on the figures produced by SCP-1045 are currently pending review.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!
short, sweet and well deserving of that rating, good job Drewbear!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if you put SCP 310 in it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EXCUSE ME SIR BUT I THINK THAT'S A POKEMON.
XD but in all seriousness, awesome yet short SCP.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is insufficient data for a meaningful reaction.

On the other hand, what's here is mechanically sound, and at least a little interesting. And so…

0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One possibility is that it's not actually the "scented" part that's leading to this, but instead the herbs or essential oils used therein to create the scent.

Were it not that it's already been contradicted by the author (obviously, spoiler!), I'd imagine that a random person gets picked every time the candle gets lit, and they experience horrific pain as the fat in their body is yanked out, replacing it with heat and fire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In addition, burning a scented candle allows the shadows to emit sound.

This killed it for me. Just from any scientific standpoint, this doesn't make sense. At a technical level, every candle is scented, even if not intentionally or with any form of industrial perfume.

Props for the unique (and undeniably creative) idea, but I'm not a huge fan of main series scips being created as a result of contests.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Sacrifice of the fresh blood :) welcome!

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
And I am grateful to the author for that final nudge. (It was at +97 for 2 months straight!)

REDbluewhiteyellow (or you shall now be known: "Mister Colorful"), my thanks.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: I do believe the tale that was just posted, unlikely though it is to survive the night, pushed this over +100. A valiant sacrifice.

Short, sweet, and unnerving.

Congrats Drew!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: May not be the original "explanation" behind this (if there is one) but in my head-canon this SCP is now responsible for spontaneous human combustion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I note that this would not work nearly so well if it were the candle rather than the lampshade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
creepy
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: bloody brilliant, can't believe how well written it is for such a short amount of time. Bravo +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Directed here from the challenges post. I can't believe you came up with something this good in 30 minutes. Part of why it's so good is the brevity, you describe the object exactly and everything about why it does this, what exactly it's doing, etc. is left to the reader…it gave me chills. Very nice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like everything but the scented candle part, doesn't feel congruent… upvoted anyway ^_^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sweet, simple, and now I can't light up my new vanilla candles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, we've already got that one. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I spent some time with a nice nutmeg candle burning as I showered this weekend. I pictured the screaming. Boring or no, , even a very short entry might be ok. The image….imagined sound?…..was unsettling. Upvote, but consider even a singular entry, perhaps two, all the same. I am of less experience here, to be sure, but even boring details sometimes add a quiet oomph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These are the kind of things I want to read when I come here. Great job :) - +1

Also:

"AVAILABLE NOW AT IKEA."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what they say: Less is More. Deliciously simple, but hellishly scary, wonderfully mysterious, and loaded with hooks.

This warrant a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic. This one has a good old-school-but-not-wanky vibe to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, creepy and mysterious.

Makes you wonder if this SCP is responsible for cases of human spontaneous combustion…

+1 of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for being short, simple and scary. It's left me wondering.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be an incredibly boring test log, believe me. All it would do is say the odor, the pungency and that yep, there's screaming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: withholding a vote for now. I'd like to see maybe at least a short experiment log, especially concerning the scented candle aspect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, sweet, and creepy as hell. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, and I know it's a response to one of Bright's "challenges", but this really does seem more like an anomalous item than an SCP. That's my only problem with it, but not enough to deny an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wrote this out really fast, so I can't say that there was some deep reasoning behind it other than I didn't want it to be that differently colored candles did it. Imagine you're the original unsuspecting owner and you put in this peppermint or lemongrass scented candle so you can relax while taking a soothing bath. Then the screaming starts.

Thanks for the upvote :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it except for the bit about scented candles. Makes it go from existential horror to satanic/occult scary story for me. Don't ask, can't explain.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. Simple yet unnerving.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely creepy. The bit about the human fat actually made me twitch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Simple, scary, and mysterious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Response to Bright's first challenge: create a SCP in under 24 hours w/o using the sandbox or getting any other input.
